notes: ROADMAP_V2 iconic-scene registry (icons #1-16) + breakpoint probes (#17-35). Every entry is a __shot spec plus meta fields (tier, exercises, note, pending, motion, disk, altLadder, fovLadder) that bench/run.mjs strips before shooting. Icons are QUALITATIVE anchors only â€” metric gates run on the rotating control set (see run.mjs --controls), never on these. Poses marked approx in notes are refinement targets for the Phase T tuning loop.

scenes.name: blue-marble
scenes.exercises: Ph1 photometry, Ph2 provinces/biomes, Ph4 clouds
scenes.note: round 14: meter spot (disc-legibility: the center meter averaged space into the target). Round 15: meter CENTER (panel blue-marble-spot-meters-cloud: with clouds shipped, a drifting cloud under the spot re-exposed the whole disc -17% and muted the icon's blue; the cloud-carried contrast makes center legible now). A documented camera-semantics change: icon re-baselined; controls keep the default meter.

scenes.note: sun ~2 deg up: the blue aureole peaks where forward-lobe blue beats path absorption (MER frames are pre-sunset). waitMs (round 17, registered r16): the alt-2m pose + Rubra's 2nd deck settles ~114 s alone but overran the 150 s default under SwiftShader sweep load — a per-scene budget, never a global bump

scenes.name: titan-saturnrise

scenes.note: band-annotated (Huygens DISR). Shadowless orange gloom, dark near-still methane sea (water.calm), rounded ice cobbles. Pose approx — refine shoreline at first-light.

scenes.note: band-annotated (Akatsuki UV disc; Venera panoramas surface tone). Descent frame just below the 48 km deck base; overcast ceiling above, orange sub-cloud twilight below.

Posed at the emergent formation cluster r14-locate.mjs found: strata-upland hoodoo/outcrop field, lat -12.849 lon -76.829 (38m outcrop + 37m hoodoos, 71 calved blocks). Slot-canyon walk: undercut/overhang shadows, formation-terrain contact, impostor handoff behind.

scenes.note: round-9 dedicated cliff pair; wide uplift gate (Rubra 0.08-0.3), flat-lying beds

scenes.note: round-10 water scene; archipelago overlook lat 9 lon -14 (bench/out/find-beach)

scenes.name: rubra-disk
scenes.exercises: Ph2 singularity (edifices, rift, consequence-chain albedo provinces)
scenes.note: round 12: the disc has a face — Tharsis-class shields, ONE rift scar, scour/mantle provinces; all emergent from the swell statistics

scenes.name: rubra-rift-oblique
scenes.exercises: Ph2 tectonism: rift profile, en-echelon side troughs, stress-boosted strata walls
scenes.note: grazing sun — the steep-wall ladder profile needs low light to read at coarse display

scenes.name: rubra-dune-sea
scenes.exercises: Ph2 coherent bedforms: crest trains along the [global] wind, defects, slip-face asymmetry, erg confinement

scenes.name: luna-wrinkle-mare
scenes.exercises: Ph2 tectonism, basin agent: concentric wrinkle ridges + margin rilles (two-body gate vs Rubra swell agent)
scenes.note: camera at the mascon center at ridge-band display altitude — higher poses settle below the stamp bands

scenes.name: tellus-megadunes
scenes.exercises: Ph2 bedforms, ice agent: Antarctic-class megadune banding on the ice sheet (anti-overfit second body)

scenes.name: luna-mascon-basin
scenes.exercises: R6 basin/mare unification (round 14)
scenes.note: round 14 FIRST-LIGHT witness for R6 (panel: the mechanism was unwitnessed by the bench). The largest ridge-bearing mascon basin (forEachBasin r=229km deg=0.87 fill=0.29, lat 68.9 lon -120.7) — round 13 instrumented mare==0 here; the fill law now floods it co-located with its deg-attenuated wrinkle ridges.

scenes.name: cloud-deck-eye
scenes.exercises: Ph4 clouds core (round 15): under/beside the deck at eye level — the tier the CORE does not yet own
scenes.note: round 15 FIRST-LIGHT, demoted to breakpoint BY MEASUREMENT: at eye level a wall 60 km out subtends <2 deg and overhead breaks need km-scale cells the 156-km raster cannot carry (the sub-raster detail is mean-1 density, not coverage erosion) — the broken-sky-overhead LOOK is round-16 content (registered: cumulus-cell erosion with mean compensation + march-window structure). This pose documents the core's honest eye-level state: veiled deck, edge glow, shadow on the water. EDGE pose from r15-locate-edge.mjs at the scene's exact t.

scenes.name: cloud-tops
scenes.exercises: Ph4 clouds core (round 15): above the deck — the core's honest above-deck state
scenes.note: round 15 FIRST-LIGHT, breakpoint BY MEASUREMENT: the core's flat-top profile renders the deck from above as a smooth stratus-like sheet (correct for the field it has) — cumulus top-height modulation + cell erosion are round-16 content (registered). The round's cloud ICONS are blue-marble and loworbit-sunset: the disc/low-orbit tier the core owns. Sun behind camera (backscatter + MS quasi-Lambert).

scenes.name: tellus-clearsky
scenes.exercises: Ph4 clouds core (round 15): the sigma(0) witness — a located clear-sky pose that must be pixel-flat clouds-on vs clouds:false
scenes.note: round 15 (panel F1-bench): both decks < 0.02 over a 500 km cap at this pose's exact t (r15-locate.mjs); the pose keeps the whole view INSIDE the cap (low alt, ground-dominated framing — the first 350 km version saw clouds 2000 km away and the AE servo moved every pixel) and pins fixedEV so the A/B isolates the integrator, not the meter. The GLSL deck integrator + shadow slot must contribute EXACTLY zero here — verified by the r15 clouds-off A/B probe; any drift is a hard gate, never an expected delta.

scenes.name: tellus-tor
scenes.exercises: Ph2 formations two-body gate (round 14 residue): the located Tellus bedrock tor at ground level
scenes.note: round 15 (round-14 panel residue): the formation two-body gate was only partially bench-testable — no ground-level Tellus outcrop scene existed. Site located by the round-14 H4 locator: a 23 m tor at -42.99/15.69 (uplift-gated outcrop agent).

scenes.name: aurora-night-disc
scenes.exercises: Ph4 §8/§10 aurora emission over the night DISC from orbit (dual-band, substorm pulse)
scenes.note: round 16 emission pack: the aurora now lives inside the one integral, so the dipole oval reads over the terrain-covered night disc from orbit (not just above the limb). Green lower + red upper bands; night clouds under the oval exercise the per-crossing deck occlusion.

scenes.name: rubra-dust-storm
scenes.exercises: Ph4 seasonal GLOBAL DUST STORM — coverage-field mode casting a butterscotch pall
scenes.note: round 16 weather config: the storm deck's cov0 is a seasonal DC lift (stormEnvelope on orbital phase). season 0.40 is near the stormLs peak; off-season Rubra is EXACTLY clear. The pall casts the ground shadow through the cloud-shadow slot and reddens the sky.

scenes.name: rubra-polar-hood
scenes.exercises: Ph4 winter-pole cloud HOOD (seasonalFrost-shape coverage term)
scenes.note: round 16: a cirrus/CO2 hood caps the winter pole (deck 0 hoodAmp). Season chosen for a winter hemisphere; refine season for max hood at first-light.

scenes.name: titan-orbit
scenes.exercises: Ph3 Titan orange organic-haze marble from orbit (betaA blue>>red)
scenes.note: round 16: validates the tholin haze colour emerges from the absorption coefficients. Dim (9.58 AU); fixedEV pinned for a stable capture (panel titan-haze-featureless).

scenes.name: venus-orbit
scenes.exercises: Ph3 Venus pale-yellow overcast marble from orbit (near-total sulfuric deck)
scenes.note: round 16: validates the near-total deck (cov0 0.95) reads as a bright pale-lemon overcast disc from space; the surface-orange lives below the deck (venus-deck-breakout).

scenes.name: vesta-orbit
scenes.exercises: Ph5 figure: oblate silhouette + crater record + Rheasilvia from orbit (round 17)
scenes.note: round 17 first figure disk: the limb is an ELLIPSE (axes 285/285/223 km — noLimb skips the circular-profile metric); the south-polar Rheasilvia basin (authored stamp, central peak) should read at this phase. Bands: crater SFD from Ph2 stamps, figure domain from figure.js.

scenes.name: vesta-rheasilvia
scenes.exercises: Ph5 figure: authored giant basin — rim, floor 19 km down, central PEAK (t=0 term)
scenes.note: round 17: standing on the Rheasilvia rim looking across — the basin spans half the body; the central peak is one of the tallest mountains anywhere. Metric-true crater footprints (3-D metres, not mean-R chord).

scenes.name: haumea-disk
scenes.exercises: Ph5 figure: EXTREME triaxial silhouette (1160x870x510 km) — the lens-shaped limb
scenes.note: round 17: the figure IS the look — a 3.9 h spinner frozen mid-rotation shows a lens, not a ball. Crystalline-ice palette + ambientAlbedo fill. noLimb: the circular limb metric is meaningless here.

scenes.name: haumea-ground
scenes.exercises: Ph5 figure: metric anisotropy on the ground (lu/lv to 2.27x) + bright-ice airless ambient
scenes.note: round 17: near the long-axis tip the per-cell metric is stretched hardest; slopes/talus/AO must read isotropic ON THE GROUND (the metric tensor working) — squashed craters here = the FD ops missed the metric. Ice shadows filled by the albedo-scaled bounce (~7x Luna's).

scenes.name: arrokoth-orbit
scenes.exercises: Ph5 figure: the contact-binary silhouette — two lobes and the waist from orbit
scenes.note: round 17: the bilobate outline (SDF union + smin neck) at ~60 km — the money silhouette. Deep-red cold-classical tholins; terminator falls across the neck at this phase.

scenes.name: arrokoth-neck
scenes.exercises: Ph5 figure: STANDING ON THE NECK — displacement along the SDF gradient, the other lobe overhead
scenes.note: round 17 money shot (pose from first-light probe fl2-neckD): standing on the neck saddle, the far lobe rising across it. The lobes are too merged for a 'lobe in the sky with stars around it' — that needs a wider gap than this figure declares (honest limit; framing polish registered). m-hat leans ~60 deg off radial at the flanks — radial machinery would bury the camera.

scenes.name: arrokoth-terminator
scenes.exercises: Ph5 figure: MUTUAL LOBE SHADOWING — one lobe's shadow crossing the neck onto the other (Sec10 slot)
scenes.note: round 17: the 8-tap min-S march in sunTransmit — lobe A occludes the sun for neck ground on lobe B near the terminator. A sphere self-limb could never cast this. Soft penumbra ~2 percent of R.

scenes.name: saturn-rings-titan
scenes.exercises: Ph6 giant + near-edge-on RINGS over Titan's dune belt (icon #14, deferred from round 16)
scenes.note: round 18: from Titan, the banded gold giant with the rings ~6deg open (season 0.35) — a thin ring ellipse showing the Cassini division + the dark RING SHADOW BAND across the disc (the money element; Saturn sub-solar lat ~-20.7deg fixed by phase0, NOT the scene season). Titan is tidally locked so Saturn stays framed; season sets the ring aspect + lighting. NO Titan inclination (the driver adjudication: pose near-edge-on, don't force exactly-edge-on).

scenes.name: saturn-disc
scenes.exercises: Ph6 giant + OPEN ring annulus (Cassini + Encke gaps) from a moon vantage
scenes.note: round 18: rings ~26deg open (season 0.15) from Titan — the full annulus with the Cassini division and Encke gap reading as dark bands, forward-scatter absent (gibbous phase). Banded gold disc + storm oval. The open shot the coplanar tree makes possible by posing (no inclination).

scenes.name: saturn-backlit
scenes.exercises: Ph6 ring FORWARD-SCATTER: backlit flare at high phase (sun behind the rings)
scenes.note: round 18: Titan on Saturn's anti-sun side (season 0.5, Saturn phase ~168deg = thin crescent) so the sun is behind the rings — the HG forward lobe brightens the ring line (a backscattering albedo mark could not). The failure signature is a ring that stays DIM here (forward-scatter inert) — the pre-code fwd-scatter-inert finding's witness scene.

scenes.name: saturn-closeup
scenes.exercises: Ph6 giant BANDS resolve (belts/zones) + storm oval at tight fov; polar hexagon is sub-resolution from Titan (honest limit)
scenes.note: round 18: Saturn ~280px at fov 18 — the zonal band structure (the load-bearing giant cue) and the storm oval resolve. The wavenumber-6 hexagon sits at the foreshortened pole (Titan is near Saturn's equatorial plane) so it reads as a polar cap smudge, not a hexagon — a documented geometric limit (the machinery is there; a pole-on vantage would resolve it).

scenes.name: europa-lineae
scenes.exercises: Ph5 cryo: the tidal LINEAE network (ruddy double ridges) + CHAOS over bright ice — the cracked-eggshell disc
scenes.note: round 18: Europa from orbit — age-rotated families of arcuate double ridges (closed-form from the polar angle, no R/lambda cliff) in ruddy contaminant over blue-white water ice, with a chaos block region. Sphere body ⇒ circular limbProfile runs (samples the fracture albedo bands). The Phase-5 fool-rate look.

scenes.name: europa-chaos
scenes.exercises: Ph5 cryo: CHAOS terrain on the ground — position-pure jostled ice rafts within the margin field
scenes.note: round 18: eye-ish level over a Conamara-class chaos region — tilted/dropped ice blocks (body-fixed-lattice hashed, seam-free, no halo diffusion). The failure signature is blocks cracking across a cube edge or popping at a LOD seam (the position-pure design forbids both). Refine lon/alt at first-light to sit on a high-margin patch.

scenes.name: pluto-sputnik
scenes.exercises: Ph5 cryo: the Sputnik-Planitia N2 GLACIER (convection cells) beside the dark tholin (Cthulhu) uplands
scenes.note: round 18: the iconic encounter hemisphere — a bright nitrogen-ice heart (authored closed-form basin + convection-cell polygons) against dark red-brown tholin (a body-fixed longitude province). Sub-observer lon/lat faces both. Sphere body ⇒ limbProfile runs. The Phase-5 fool-rate look.

scenes.name: pluto-blades
scenes.exercises: Ph5 cryo: sublimation BLADES/pits on the glacier (oriented on the recipe mean-insolation axis, §5 sun-independent)
scenes.note: round 18: low over the Sputnik glacier — penitente-like blade ridges + sublimation pits, oriented by a recipe axis (the bake is sun-independent). Convection-cell troughs at coarser scale. Failure signature: blade orientation keying on the live sun (it must not) or a LOD onset step.

scenes.name: cinis-perihelion
scenes.note: Phase S Mercury-family eccentricity and lobate-scarp data anchor.

scenes.name: iovis-family
scenes.note: Galilean-analog family portrait from Sulcus; the Laplace group is live data.

scenes.name: caelus-sideways
scenes.note: Parent-facing Ruina pose at maximum 4.34-degree ring opening resolves Caelus's 98-degree pole and faint ring without the edge-on conditioning floor.

scenes.name: pontus-storm
scenes.note: Parent-facing Errans pose exercises Pontus through the live giant-band and storm path.

scenes.name: pluto-navita-dance
scenes.note: Mutual Pluto-Charon analog around the authored barycenter; Navita carries the polar tholin knob.

scenes.name: cinis-day
scenes.note: Phase W showcase: the 3:2 lockRatio makes the solar day two cinis years (K3); at the day/s detent a full sunrise-to-sunrise is ~176 real seconds. Declared warp pins the above-band representation set for the frozen capture.

scenes.name: perihelion-seasons
scenes.note: Phase W showcase: epoch solved for the storm deck's Ls 4.71 (true-anomaly keyed since Phase S re-keying); declared month/s warp renders the Jensen-honest season-local cloud mean — the dusty pall as a time-average, not a strobe.
